mechanism_narrative: A falling federal funds rate reduces discount rates and supports equity valuations, so a negative change in FEDFUNDS over the prior month predicts positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days. The signal fails when rate cuts are fully expected and do not surprise markets.
failure_condition: If negative monthly changes in FEDFUNDS do not outperform unchanged or rising FEDFUNDS on 20-day SPX returns, or the spread in mean forward returns is not positive in at least 2 of 3 rolling years, reject the hypothesis.

mechanism_narrative: An unemployment rate break above a rising threshold marks labor-market deterioration that increases recession odds and compresses risk appetite, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days. The effect is strongest when UNRATE is trending higher, not merely elevated.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises by more than 0.3 percentage points over 3 months
failure_condition: Reject if triggered UNRATE rises are followed by positive 20-day SPX returns in more than 60% of cases, or if the average triggered-return spread is not negative versus non-triggered periods.

mechanism_narrative: A synchronized improvement in real activity and household demand, captured by rising PAYEMS and RSAFS, signals broad revenue support and stronger earnings expectations, which lifts S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days. The mechanism fails when employment gains are offset by weak sales growth.
evidence_refs: fact:latest PAYEMS and RSAFS snapshots remain above prior revised values
failure_condition: Reject if the joint condition of rising PAYEMS and rising RSAFS does not produce higher 60-day SPX returns than either series alone, or if the interaction term is not positive in walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: A manufacturing downturn shown by falling INDPRO indicates weaker industrial profits and slower cyclicals, which predicts negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days. The signal is strongest when industrial production momentum is negative for at least two consecutive months.
failure_condition: If consecutive monthly declines in INDPRO fail to precede negative 5-day SPX returns more often than not, or if the average forward return under the trigger is not below zero, reject the hypothesis.

mechanism_narrative: When CPI inflation is elevated while the policy rate remains restrictive, equity multiples compress because real rates stay high and investors expect tighter financial conditions to persist. A high CPIAUCSL trend together with FEDFUNDS above 4.5% predicts negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if high-inflation/high-rate trigger states do not produce negative 20-day SPX returns relative to all other states, or if the interaction coefficient is not negative in rolling out-of-sample tests.

mechanism_narrative: A rising PAYEMS trend indicates improving payroll growth and household income, which supports consumption and earnings expectations, leading to positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days. The signal fails when payroll gains are purely temporary and are reversed in the following quarter.
failure_condition: Reject if upward PAYEMS momentum does not outperform downward momentum on 60-day SPX returns, or if positive PAYEMS changes are followed by negative average returns over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A falling CPIAUCSL combined with stable or easing FEDFUNDS lowers real policy pressure and raises the probability of multiple expansion, so the pair predicts positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days. The signal is strongest when inflation decelerates faster than the policy rate.
failure_condition: Reject if disinflation episodes paired with unchanged or falling FEDFUNDS do not beat other regimes on 20-day SPX returns, or if the interaction effect is statistically indistinguishable from zero.

mechanism_narrative: In a post-recession recovery, rising HOUST signals improving cyclicality, easier credit transmission, and future construction activity, which supports S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days. The mechanism fails when housing starts rise without concurrent improvement in broader demand.
candidate_trigger: HOUST rises by more than 5% from its 3-month moving average
failure_condition: Reject if HOUST breakouts do not precede positive 5-day SPX returns more often than baseline, or if the breakout signal turns negative in any sustained 2-year evaluation window.
